reasoning: The value on 2025-04-27 was 664.45, which represents a highly elevated outlier compared to the recent mean (around 450 in the second half of April 2025) and historical norms (around 100-200 in early 2024). Looking at the past 15 days, only one day (910.15 on Apr 13) exceeded this threshold, giving a recent base rate of ~6.7% for days > 664.45. As a daily index reflecting news counts, the USEPUINDXD tends to show mean reversion following acute spikes in policy-related news. Therefore, the probability that the index will exceed 664.45 on a specifically chosen future day is quite low. I estimate the probability of exceeding the threshold on May 4 is around 15%. Over longer time horizons, while the fundamental base rate of a >664 spike might actually decline as current political shocks stabilize, I follow the explicit instruction to regress probabilities toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon increases to account for greater structural uncertainty. Thus, the estimates increase smoothly toward 0.5 for the later dates.
